name: Data Science
description: An interdisciplinary program combining statistics, mathematics, programming, and domain expertise to extract insights and knowledge from structured and unstructured data.

subjects.name: Probability
subjects.description: Fundamentals of probability theory including random variables, probability distributions, expected values, variance, conditional probability, Bayes theorem, and law of large numbers.
learning_outcomes: Apply probability axioms and Bayes theorem to real-world problems
Work with discrete and continuous probability distributions
Compute expectations, variances, and moments of random variables
Apply law of large numbers and central limit theorem
subjects.industry_relevance: Probability is fundamental to risk assessment, financial modeling, and the foundation of all machine learning algorithms.

subjects.name: Statistics
subjects.description: Statistical methods for data analysis including descriptive statistics, inferential statistics, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, ANOVA, and non-parametric tests.
learning_outcomes: Summarize data using descriptive statistics and visualizations
Perform hypothesis tests and interpret p-values and confidence intervals
Apply ANOVA for comparing multiple groups
Select appropriate statistical tests for different data types
subjects.industry_relevance: Statistical literacy is essential for data-driven decision making in healthcare, finance, marketing, and research.

subjects.name: Data Analysis
subjects.description: Practical techniques for exploring, cleaning, transforming, and analyzing data using Python. Covers data wrangling, exploratory data analysis, statistical modeling, and reporting.
learning_outcomes: Clean and preprocess real-world messy datasets
Perform exploratory data analysis to uncover patterns and outliers
Apply statistical modeling techniques for inference
Communicate findings through reports and dashboards
subjects.industry_relevance: Data analysis is the core function of data teams across e-commerce, finance, healthcare, and technology companies.

subjects.name: Data Mining
subjects.description: Techniques for discovering patterns in large datasets including association rule mining, clustering, classification, anomaly detection, and sequence pattern mining.
learning_outcomes: Implement Apriori and FP-Growth for frequent pattern mining
Apply clustering algorithms including k-means and hierarchical clustering
Detect anomalies using statistical and distance-based methods
Mine sequential patterns from time-series and transaction data
subjects.industry_relevance: Data mining drives customer analytics, fraud detection, market basket analysis, and recommendation systems.

subjects.name: Big Data Analytics
subjects.description: Technologies for analyzing massive datasets using distributed computing. Covers Hadoop, Spark, MapReduce, Hive, and real-time analytics with stream processing frameworks.
learning_outcomes: Process large datasets using Apache Spark DataFrames and RDDs
Implement MapReduce algorithms for distributed computation
Build real-time analytics pipelines with Apache Kafka and Spark Streaming
Query big data using Hive and Presto
subjects.industry_relevance: Big data analytics is critical for organizations processing terabytes of data daily in finance, social media, and IoT domains.

subjects.name: Data Visualization
subjects.description: Principles and techniques for effective data visualization. Covers visual perception, chart types, dashboard design, and tools including Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly, Tableau, and D3.js.
learning_outcomes: Design compelling visualizations following best practices in visual perception
Build interactive dashboards using Tableau or Plotly Dash
Create custom visualizations with D3.js
Communicate data stories effectively to stakeholders
subjects.industry_relevance: Data visualization is essential for business reporting, executive dashboards, and communicating insights across organizations.

subjects.name: Machine Learning
subjects.description: Comprehensive machine learning course covering supervised and unsupervised learning, model evaluation, feature engineering, regularization, and ensemble methods using Scikit-learn.
learning_outcomes: Build regression and classification models using various algorithms
Apply feature engineering and feature selection techniques
Implement ensemble methods including random forests and gradient boosting
Deploy ML models using Flask or FastAPI
subjects.industry_relevance: Machine learning is the core technology behind recommendation engines, predictive analytics, and intelligent automation.

subjects.name: Deep Learning
subjects.description: Advanced neural network architectures for data science applications. Covers CNNs for structured data, RNNs for sequences, autoencoders for feature learning, and transfer learning.
learning_outcomes: Design neural networks for tabular and time-series data
Implement autoencoders for dimensionality reduction and anomaly detection
Apply transfer learning for data-scarce problems
Optimize deep learning models using hyperparameter tuning
subjects.industry_relevance: Deep learning enables advanced predictive analytics in finance, healthcare, and e-commerce with state-of-the-art accuracy.

subjects.name: Business Analytics
subjects.description: Application of data analytics in business contexts covering descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics. Includes customer analytics, financial analytics, and supply chain analytics.
learning_outcomes: Apply analytics frameworks to solve business problems
Perform customer segmentation and churn analysis
Build financial forecasting and budgeting models
Optimize business processes using prescriptive analytics
subjects.industry_relevance: Business analytics drives strategy and operations in consulting, retail, banking, and technology companies worldwide.

subjects.name: Predictive Analytics
subjects.description: Techniques for building predictive models using historical data. Covers time series analysis, survival analysis, forecasting methods, and causal inference for business and scientific applications.
learning_outcomes: Build ARIMA, Prophet, and LSTM models for time series forecasting
Apply survival analysis for customer churn and event prediction
Design A/B tests and perform causal inference from observational data
Evaluate predictive model performance using backtesting
subjects.industry_relevance: Predictive analytics is widely used in finance for trading, healthcare for patient outcomes, and retail for demand forecasting.

subjects.name: SQL
subjects.description: Structured Query Language for database management and data manipulation. Covers DDL, DML, complex queries, joins, subqueries, window functions, views, indexes, and query optimization.
learning_outcomes: Write complex SQL queries with joins, subqueries, and CTEs
Apply window functions for analytical queries
Design and optimize database schemas for analytical workloads
Perform ETL operations using SQL
subjects.industry_relevance: SQL is the most universal skill for data professionals used across all companies for data extraction and analysis.

subjects.name: Data Warehousing
subjects.description: Design and implementation of data warehouses for business intelligence. Covers dimensional modeling, star and snowflake schemas, ETL processes, OLAP operations, and cloud data warehouses.
learning_outcomes: Design star and snowflake dimensional models
Build ETL pipelines using Apache Airflow or SSIS
Implement OLAP cubes for multidimensional analysis
Deploy data warehouses on cloud platforms like Snowflake or Redshift
subjects.industry_relevance: Data warehousing is the backbone of enterprise business intelligence and reporting infrastructure.

subjects.name: Python for Data Science
subjects.description: Python programming specifically for data science workflows. Covers NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Jupyter notebooks for end-to-end data analysis.
learning_outcomes: Manipulate and analyze data using Pandas DataFrames
Perform numerical computations with NumPy
Create publication-quality visualizations with Matplotlib and Seaborn
Build and evaluate ML models using Scikit-learn pipelines
subjects.industry_relevance: Python is the lingua franca of data science, used by data professionals in every industry for analysis and modeling.
